svg.title: Overlapping clinical phenotypes in genes associated with monogenic forms of autism spectrum disorder (ASD), dystonia, epilepsy and schizophrenia

cc:Work.dc:title: Overlapping clinical phenotypes in genes associated with monogenic forms of autism spectrum disorder (ASD), dystonia, epilepsy and schizophrenia
cc:Work.dc:description: Euler diagram showing overlapping clinical phenotypes in genes associated with monogenic forms of autism spectrum disorder (ASD), dystonia, epilepsy and schizophrenia. Blue indicates genes associated with epilepsy, green indicates genes associated with schizophrenia, purple indicates genes associated with autism spectrum disorder (ASD), and red indicates genes associated with dystonia. Associated disorders are caused by highly penetrant single gene variants. ADHD, attention deficit hyperactivity disorder; OCD, obsessive–compulsive disorder.
